description:
Step 4 of T-012's 4-step CSP roadmap. **Status: BLOCKED pending inline-handler migration completion.**

## Blocking issues

This ticket cannot ship until all three close:

- **ISS-020** — fitting_tool.html innerHTML refactor (36 inline handlers embedded in JS string-concat).
- **ISS-021** — define `data-click-arg` dispatcher contract for arg-bearing handlers.
- **ISS-022** — per-template migration sweep (~150 sites including 7 stopProp+arg holdouts).

ISS-023 (CSP report endpoint) is a strong nice-to-have for automated soak-period diffing but is not a strict blocker; manual DevTools scans work too.

(Note: storybloq's structured `blockedBy` field only accepts ticket IDs, not issue IDs. Until/unless these are promoted to T-* tickets, the structural field stays empty and this description is the authoritative blocker record.)

## Why it's blocked

T-033's "Done when" gate is *"zero CSP violations for inline event handlers across the major routes."* As of 2026-05-19, the repo carries **184 inline event handlers across 52 templates** (verified by grep). Flipping `Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only` → `Content-Security-Policy` (enforcing) now would block every one of them, breaking major flows: fitting tool drag-drop and module add, gatecheck route search, structure-timer edits, skill plan reordering, mining ledger interactions, admin user management, intel D-scan submit, etc.

## What CAN'T safely ship as an intermediate step

Dropping `'unsafe-inline'` from `script-src` while keeping Report-Only is technically safe (CSP3 nonces take precedence, so modern browsers are already nonce-enforced). But it produces ZERO new information: Report-Only with-or-without `'unsafe-inline'` is observationally indistinguishable on CSP3 browsers. We'd be pre-committing a design decision that has to be re-verified at flip time regardless. Bundle it with the full enforcing flip.

## Executable runway (for the future session)

1. **Two-stage grep gate** — template-only is insufficient; broad event-name coverage matters because a missed handler post-flip is a production outage:
   - `grep -rE 'on[a-z]+=' app/templates/` → expect zero (excluding the `data-on-error="hide"` shortcut which is `data-*`, not `on*`). The broad `on[a-z]+=` catches drag/drop/paste/context-menu/toggle/invalid handlers that a narrow event-name list misses.
   - `grep -rE "on[a-z]+=" app/static/js/ --include='*.js'` plus visual scan of `<script>` blocks inside templates for HTML-string concatenation that embeds `on*=` attrs (the fitting_tool.html innerHTML pattern from ISS-020).
2. **Soak under Report-Only** for at least 24–48h after the last migration lands. ISS-023 lands first if available; otherwise manual route walk-through.
3. Edit `app/middleware/csp_nonce.py:_CSP_TEMPLATE`: drop `'unsafe-inline'` from `script-src`. Keep `style-src 'unsafe-inline'` per T-032's decision. Rename `Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only` → `Content-Security-Policy`.
4. SSH to VPS, edit `/opt/edge/nginx/conf.d/vigilant.conf:47`, remove the static Report-Only header line. `docker exec edge-nginx-1 nginx -s reload`.
5. Hard-refresh each major route in DevTools — zero CSP violations. Test htmx-loaded partials (fitting search results, gatecheck routes, wormhole kills, admin users, structure timer edits). All actions must work.
6. **Rollback path:** `git revert <sha> && deploy.sh` + restore the nginx line on the VPS.

## Done

Closes VVP-2026-007 after deploy + verification.
title: CSP Step 4: drop unsafe-inline + flip Report-Only to enforcing (VVP-2026-007)
